title: Habenaria × evansii
taxonomy: Habenaria × evansii is a hybrid orchid species, classified under the genus Habenaria. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest family of flowering plants. The species is further categorized under the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Cranichideae. Its classification makes it a part of the orchid family, which includes over 30,000 known species. This classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics.

intro: Habenaria × evansii, also known as Evans' Habenaria, is a hybrid orchid species that belongs to the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of infections. The bioactive constituents of Habenaria × evansii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional medicine and is prepared in various herbal forms, including capsules, teas, and tinctures, to harness its purported health benefits.
